name: Irish Coffee Cake
recipeInstructions:
-------------
1. Prepare the Irish coffee syrup by combining 150 ml strong black coffee and 4 ounces of sugar in a small saucepan over medium heat until the sugar dissolves. Add 2 tablespoons Irish whiskey or an equivalent amount, then simmer for 1 minute before removing from heat.
2. Grease and flour an 8-inch cake pan (preferably a springform cheesecake pan).
3. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
4. In a large bowl, cream together the butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Add the eggs one at a time, mixing well after each addition. Stir in the coffee essence and mix thoroughly.
5. Gradually fold in the self-raising flour into the batter, ensuring it is well combined.
6. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake for 35-40 minutes until set and springy to the touch.
7. Allow the cake to cool completely on a wire rack.
8. To glaze the cake, pour the hot Irish coffee syrup over the cooled cake in its baking pan, allowing it to soak in for several hours before transferring it back.
9. Whip 150 ml heavy whipping cream until thickened, sweeten with a few tablespoons of confectioners' sugar, and add a tablespoon or two of whiskey to taste.
10. Spread the whipped cream over the cooled cake and refrigerate for at least one hour before decorating with chopped nuts or grated chocolate.
